nodes.context: Campanian Camorra clan operating in Naples, one of the most powerful Camorra groups.
Camorra clan headed by Michele Zaza, relative of the Mazzarella brothers.
Camorra clan, part of the Secondigliano Alliance, rival of Mazzarella clan.
Camorra clan allied with Mazzarella and Sarno clans in the Misso-Mazzarella-Sarno alliance.
Camorra clan allied with Mazzarella and Misso clans; fell in 2008.
Alliance of Camorra clans including Licciardi, Mallardo, and Contini bosses.

Camorra clan, part of Secondigliano Alliance, rival of Mazzarella clan.

Camorra clan, long-time rival of Mazzarella clan.
Sicilian Mafia family from Catania, allied with Ciro Mazzarella.
Camorra organization led by Vincenzo Casillo; Mazzarella did not take sides in its war with Nuova Famiglia.
Camorra alliance; Michele Zaza held a top position.
Criminal groups from Albania, allied with Mazzarella clan according to DIA.

edges.evidence_quote: The clan was founded in the 1960s by the Mazzarella brothers, Ciro known as 'o Scellone, Gennaro and Vincenzo, as a branch of the Zaza clan, then headed by the historical boss Michele Zaza, who was a relative of the Mazzarella brothers.
edges.context: Mazzarella clan originated as a branch of the Zaza clan.

edges.evidence_quote: during the eighties and nineties the organization ended up involved in many Camorra wars, in particular against the Contini clan, which led to the death of dozens of affiliates including the father of the Mazzarella brothers, Francesco, killed in an ambush in 1998
edges.context: Mazzarella clan fought wars against the Contini clan, resulting in deaths.

edges.evidence_quote: In the late 1980s the Mazzarellas formed a strong alliance with the Misso and Sarno clans, called Misso-Mazzarella-Sarno, to oppose to the rising power of the Secondigliano Alliance
edges.context: Mazzarella clan allied with Misso and Sarno clans in the Misso-Mazzarella-Sarno alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: In the late 1980s the Mazzarellas formed a strong alliance with the Misso and Sarno clans, called Misso-Mazzarella-Sarno, to oppose to the rising power of the Secondigliano Alliance
edges.context: Mazzarella clan allied with Misso and Sarno clans in the Misso-Mazzarella-Sarno alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: In the late 1980s the Mazzarellas formed a strong alliance with the Misso and Sarno clans, called Misso-Mazzarella-Sarno, to oppose to the rising power of the Secondigliano Alliance, headed by the Licciardi, Mallardo and Contini bosses.
edges.context: Mazzarella clan opposed the Secondigliano Alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1996 Marianna, daughter of Luigi Giuliano, boss of the Giuliano clan, married Michele Mazzarella, son of Vincenzo Mazzarella, the union served to strengthen the relations between the two most important clans of Camorra in the 1990s.
edges.context: Marriage between Giuliano and Mazzarella families strengthened relations between the two clans.

edges.evidence_quote: The clan has a long history of rivalry with the Rinaldi clan, which caused dozens of deaths from the bloody wars between the two groups.
edges.context: Mazzarella clan has a long-standing rivalry with the Rinaldi clan, resulting in many deaths.

edges.evidence_quote: Ciro Mazzarella ... having strong relations with the Sicilian Mafia, in particular with the Catania Mafia family.
edges.context: Ciro Mazzarella had strong relations with the Catania Mafia family.

edges.evidence_quote: He was also seen in the company of Vincenzo Casillo of the Nuova Camorra Organizzata, in fact, Mazzarella never took a side in the war between the Nuova Famiglia and the NCO
edges.context: Mazzarella was seen with NCO boss but remained neutral in the NF-NCO war.

edges.evidence_quote: According to the Direzione Investigativa Antimafia, the clan has alliances with Albanian mafia groups.
edges.context: Mazzarella clan has alliances with Albanian mafia groups.

edges.evidence_quote: In recent years, the new leaders of the Russo clan, from Nola, also formed an alliance with the Mazzarellas.
edges.context: Russo clan formed an alliance with Mazzarella clan in recent years.

edges.evidence_quote: The De Bernardo group (led by Rosario De Bernardo, brother of Vincenzo 'o pisello', murdered in 2015) handled large-scale distribution of cocaine, crack, marijuana, and hashish, primarily in Somma Vesuviana.
edges.context: De Bernardo group is a satellite group operating under Mazzarella clan's command.

edges.evidence_quote: The Anastasio group (led by Raffaele Anastasio) ran extortion rackets targeting local businesses in Sant’Anastasia to fund clan operations and support imprisoned members.
edges.context: Anastasio group is a satellite group operating under Mazzarella clan's command.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Russo clan, Catania Mafia family, Ndrangheta, South American drug cartels
edges.context: Infobox lists Ndrangheta as an ally of Mazzarella clan.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Russo clan, Catania Mafia family, Ndrangheta, South American drug cartels
edges.context: Infobox lists South American drug cartels as allies of Mazzarella clan.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Secondigliano Alliance Contini clan, Licciardi clan, Mallardo clan, Rinaldi clan, De Luca Bossa clan, Vollaro clan, Aprea-Cuccaro clan
edges.context: Infobox lists Licciardi clan as a rival of Mazzarella clan.

edges.context: Infobox lists Mallardo clan as a rival of Mazzarella clan.
